────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
x.18°E PHASE ARTIFACT

  Every longitude of the form x.18°E (x = integer) produces the maximum
  A+ count (92) because these points are equidistant from all harmonics
  of the 0.1-beru grid.  There are 120 such anchors (one per 3° = 0.1 beru).
  They are uninformative: every historically attested x.18° site would score
  identically regardless of cultural significance.

  Gerizim's phase: 2.269° mod 3°  (offset 0.089° from x.18°)
  Jerusalem's phase: 2.2317° mod 3°  (offset 0.0517° from x.18°)

  The ranking table above groups all 120 x.18° anchors at rank 1 since
  they are indistinguishable and irrelevant to the Levantine corridor question.
